mission.title: Install and Configure Ollama
mission.description: Ollama is an AI platform designed for serving large language models (LLMs) like Kimi-K2.5 and GLM-5. It simplifies the deployment and management of AI models in Kubernetes environments, making it suitable for developers looking to integrate AI capabilities into their applications. Key trade-offs include the need for adequate resources and potential costs associated with running multiple models.

steps.title: Install the Ollama CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Ollama command-line interface (CLI) on your local machine, which is necessary for interacting with the Ollama platform.

```bash

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

irm https://ollama.com/install.ps1 | iex

```

> Use this command for Linux and macOS.
> Use this command for Windows.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster on a managed service provider with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create ollama-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command fetches the credentials for your cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy Ollama on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Ollama application on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add ollama https://ollama.com/charts

helm install ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.6 --namespace ollama --create-namespace

```

> Add the Ollama Helm chart repository.
> Install Ollama in its own namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ollama
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Ollama deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if persistent volumes are not handled properly.

```bash

helm uninstall ollama --namespace ollama

kubectl delete namespace ollama

```

> This command removes the Ollama release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ollama
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This process will upgrade the Ollama installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.6 --namespace ollama

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade the Ollama release.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Ollama Docker image is not available or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your internet connection and ensure the image exists: 'docker pull ollama/ollama:0.17.6'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ollama pod is failing to start, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the Ollama deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes API server unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or if the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Verify your kubeconfig with 'kubectl config view' and ensure the cluster is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models including Kimi-K2.5 and GLM-5, improved API performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n ollama' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
